creator: Net147@hotmail.com
text:
In KDE Control Center, Internet & Network -> Samba:
The SAMBA configuration file 'smb.conf' could not be found;

make sure you have SAMBA installed.


Clicking on Specify Location and entering /usr/local/etc/smb.conf allows you to temporarily access SAMBA settings but the location doesn't seem to be used next time you try to access Samba settings after starting KDE Control Center again.

Fix: 

A temporary fix:
ln -s /usr/local/etc/smb.conf /etc/smb.conf

Though it would be better to actually get it fixed properly.
How-To-Repeat: Start KDE Control Center (kcontrol), expand Internet & Network, click Samba.
